steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which sentence best emphasizes a similarity between The Three Passions and Désirée. The shared feature is that both films include Merle Oberon.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "*The Three Passions* and *Désirée* are both films that include Merle Oberon, the first Indian-born actress to be nominated for an Academy Award.": Uses 'both' and names the shared feature directly. ✓
  • "*The Three Passions* (1928) was released early in actress Merle Oberon's career, whereas *Désirée* (1954) came out later.": Contrast language highlights a difference, not a similarity. ✗
  • "In *The Three Passions* (1928), actress Merle Oberon played a nameless, uncredited role; however, in *Désirée* (1954), she played a credited role—that of Empress Josephine.": Same problem — signals contrast rather than similarity. ✗
  • "Early in her career, Merle Oberon wasn't listed in some film credits, such as the credits for the film *The Three Passions*, where she played a nameless, uncredited role.": Cannot emphasize a similarity when only one film is discussed. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
